description: FAIL-CLOSED for a Promise-returning stdlib call (scrml:auth verifyPassword) inside a `.sort` async COMPARATOR of a server function. Phase-2 colorless-async (DD colorless-async-boundaries, ratified S259 — 'build the async collection') BUILT the async-callback combinator transform for the CLEAN FAMILY (some/every/find/findIndex/filter/map/forEach/reduce/flatMap): those now lower to `await _scrml_<method>Async(coll, asyncCb)` instead of failing closed. `.sort` is FORK 2 — an async merge-sort is O(n log n) async comparisons with no native sort reuse (high complexity, low value), so it stays fail-closed. `await` is illegal in the sync comparator body, and scrml has no source `await` (§13.1), so a BARE emission returns an unawaited Promise — always truthy → the comparator always returns -1, silently corrupting the sort. The compiler fails closed with E-ASYNC-STDLIB-IN-SYNC-CALLBACK instead of shipping the leak.
rationale: §13.2 Compiler-Managed Asynchrony: the compiler SHALL insert `await` at every server call site where a Promise-returning call is made. The clean-family collection callbacks are lowered to async-aware combinators (a bounded, order-and-early-exit-preserving rewrite — DD FORK 1). `.sort` has no such bounded transform (async merge-sort, no native reuse — DD FORK 2), so a Promise-returning stdlib call in its comparator cannot be auto-awaited and a bare emission is a silent-wrong sort. §49 no-silent-bad-output requires a fail-closed hard error (E-ASYNC-STDLIB-IN-SYNC-CALLBACK, §34) — the async-stdlib sibling of the peer-server-fn E-SERVER-FN-IN-SYNC-CALLBACK. Resolution: precompute the async values into a field in a `for` loop, then sort synchronously.
